Module com.trifasico.x50zo
Package com.trifasico.x50zo.controller

Class MachineThinkingTask

java.lang.Object
java.util.concurrent.FutureTask<V>
javafx.concurrent.Task<Void>
com.trifasico.x50zo.controller.MachineThinkingTask
All Implemented Interfaces:
Runnable, Future<Void>, RunnableFuture<Void>, javafx.concurrent.Worker<Void>, javafx.event.EventTarget
public class MachineThinkingTask extends javafx.concurrent.Task<Void>
A JavaFX Task that simulates a machine player "thinking" before acting, running the decision and play logic on a background thread.

Threading model

JavaFX requires that all scene-graph mutations happen on the Application Thread. Task runs its call() method on a worker thread managed by a ExecutorService or a plain Thread. This class therefore:

  1. Sleeps for THINKING_MILLIS ms on the worker thread to create the illusion of deliberation.
  2. Wraps the actual play inside Platform.runLater so that TurnManager.machinePlayCard() — which mutates model state and fires GameEventListener callbacks that update the UI — executes on the Application Thread.

Usage


 MachineThinkingTask task = new MachineThinkingTask(turnManager);
 task.setOnFailed(e -> handleError(task.getException()));
 new Thread(task).start();

Errors that occur inside Platform.runLater cannot be caught by setOnFailed; they are instead forwarded to onPlayError(Exception) which subclasses or lambdas can override.

Version:
1.0
Author:
Yostin Ramirez, Lesly Zapata, Joseph Terreros

  • Field Details

    • THINKING_MILLIS

      public static final long THINKING_MILLIS
      Milliseconds the task sleeps before executing the machine's play.
      See Also:
      • Constant Field Values

  • Constructor Details

    • MachineThinkingTask

      public MachineThinkingTask(TurnManager turnManager)
      Constructs a MachineThinkingTask for the given game session.
      Parameters:
      turnManager - the active game coordinator; must not be null
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if turnManager is null

  • Method Details

    • call

      protected Void call() throws InterruptedException
      Sleeps for THINKING_MILLIS ms, then schedules the machine's play on the JavaFX Application Thread via Platform.runLater.

      This method runs on a worker thread. It returns null (Void) because its result is communicated through the TurnManager's event listener rather than through the task's value.

      Specified by:
      call in class javafx.concurrent.Task<Void>
      Returns:
      null
      Throws:
      InterruptedException - if the sleep is interrupted, which marks the task as failed via JavaFX Task machinery

    • onPlayError

      protected void onPlayError(Exception e)
      Called on the JavaFX Application Thread when an unexpected checked exception occurs during TurnManager.machinePlayCard().

      The default implementation re-throws the exception wrapped in a RuntimeException so the error surfaces visibly during development. Override this method in the controller to show a dialog or gracefully end the game instead.

      Parameters:
      e - the exception thrown by the turn manager
